question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value pointing all node entries to a single attacker-controlled IP:port, causing the rosetta service to route all Hiero network traffic exclusively to that endpoint and partition it from the legitimate network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES string with duplicate node keys mapping to conflicting AccountIDs, causing the NodeMap to silently overwrite legitimate node entries and produce a reduced node set that partitions the service from a subset of the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a single malformed entry that passes the len(parts) != 3 check (e.g., '0.0.0.0:50211:0.0.3') but resolves to an unreachable address, causing the entire NodeMap to be replaced with one unreachable node and fully partitioning the service from the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value with an empty string between commas (e.g., '192.168.0.1:50211:0.0.3,,192.168.0.2:50211:0.0.4'), causing strings.Split to produce an empty token that bypasses the parts-length check and corrupts the NodeMap, leading to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a very large number of node entries (e.g., thousands of entries) to exhaust memory during NodeMap construction in nodeMapDecodeHookFunc(), causing the service to crash and become partitioned from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to entries with loopback addresses (127.0.0.1:50211:0.0.3), causing the service to route all Hiero consensus node traffic to localhost and effectively partition itself from the real Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to entries with port 0 (e.g., '192.168.0.1:0:0.0.3'), which passes the 3-part split check but results in an invalid port, causing connection failures to all nodes and a complete network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to entries using IPv6 addresses that contain colons (e.g., '::1:50211:0.0.3'), causing strings.Split on ':' to produce more than 3 parts and triggering an error that aborts LoadConfig(), partitioning the service from the network on startup?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value where all AccountIDs reference non-existent accounts (e.g., '0.0.99999999'), causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to succeed but the resulting NodeMap to reference invalid consensus nodes, partitioning the service from the real Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to entries with unreachable RFC-1918 addresses (e.g., '10.255.255.1:50211:0.0.3'), causing the service to replace all legitimate node endpoints with unreachable private addresses and partition itself from the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path pointing to a YAML file with a nodes section mapping all node keys to attacker-controlled endpoints, causing mergeExternalConfigFile() to override the default node configuration and partition the service from the legitimate Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path of a world-readable file on the server that contains a malformed YAML nodes block, causing mergeExternalConfigFile() to return an error that aborts startup and partitions the service from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path of a YAML file that sets the Network field to a non-existent network name (e.g., 'fakenet'), causing strings.ToLower() to normalize it and the service to attempt connecting to an undefined network, effectively partitioning it from the real Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets NodeRefreshInterval to an extremely large duration (e.g., '87600h'), causing the service to never refresh its node list and remain partitioned from any network topology changes for years?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets NodeRefreshInterval to zero or a negative duration, causing the node refresh logic to behave unexpectedly (e.g., constant refresh loops or no refresh), disrupting the service's ability to maintain a consistent view of the Hiero network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path of a YAML file that sets the Nodes map to an empty map, causing mergeExternalConfigFile() to merge an empty node list over the defaults and leave the service with no reachable consensus nodes, partitioning it from the Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets Online to false, causing the service to enter offline mode and refuse to process live network requests, effectively partitioning it from the Hiero network from the perspective of all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets the Db.Host to an unreachable address, causing the service to lose its database connection and be unable to serve any network state queries, effectively partitioning it from the Hiero network data layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a YAML file that sets Db.Port to 0 or an invalid port, causing the GetDsn() to produce an invalid DSN and the service to fail all database connections, partitioning it from its data source and the Hiero network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to a different network name (e.g., 'mainnet' when the service is intended for 'testnet'), causing v.AutomaticEnv() to override the network field and route the service to the wrong Hiero network, partitioning it from its intended network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES via environment variable after HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG is processed, exploiting the fact that v.AutomaticEnv() is called after file merging, to override a legitimate node configuration with attacker-controlled endpoints and partition the service from the real Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_ONLINE to 'false' via environment variable, exploiting v.AutomaticEnv() to override the Online field and force the service into offline mode, partitioning it from the live Hiero network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_PORT to an already-in-use port number via environment variable, causing the service to fail to bind and become unreachable, effectively partitioning it from all network clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_HTTP_IDLETIMEOUT to '1ns' via environment variable, causing the Http.IdleTimeout to be set to near-zero and all idle HTTP connections to be immediately dropped, partitioning clients from the service through connection exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_HTTP_READTIMEOUT to '1ns' via environment variable, causing Http.ReadTimeout to be near-zero and all incoming HTTP requests to time out before being read, effectively partitioning the service from all network clients?
